content.full_text:
October 23, 1991

SCOTT HALLABRIN, ESQ.
ACTING GENERAL COUNSEL
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

By way of introduction, I am the City Attorney for the City of Fountain Valley.

Recently, I have had a series of discussions with Blanca Breeze of your office about Regulations 18700 and 18704.6, and the effect of Senate Bill 883. Although our discussion initially related to consultants paid for by the City from developer contributions, I then asked if, in the context of an environmental consultant who might prepare an E.I.R. or similar document, there was not a F.P.P.C. source of income problem if the developer directly hired the consultant. (The City would then exercise its independent judgment about any report thereby produced.)

Would you kindly provide written confirmation advice that there is no F.P.P.C. problem with such an arrangement?

Thank you for your assistance in this matter.

Very truly yours,

Alan R. Burns
City Attorney
